title: Save System — Database Persistence
description: Create a GameDatabase class using sqlite3 for persistent storage. Create tables for players (name, health, room, gold) and inventory (player_name, item_id). Implement save_player(), load_player(), save_world_state(), and load_world_state() methods. Tables should be auto-created on initialization.
test_code:
import sqlite3 as _sqlite3
db = GameDatabase(':memory:')
assert db._conn is not None, 'GameDatabase should open a connection on init'
assert db.db_path == ':memory:', 'db_path should be stored'
p = Player('TestHero', 'Dungeon', 85, ['axe', 'shield'], 120)
db.save_player(p)
loaded = db.load_player('TestHero')
assert loaded is not None, 'load_player should return a Player object'
assert loaded.name == 'TestHero', f"Expected 'TestHero', got '{loaded.name}'"
assert loaded.current_room == 'Dungeon', f"Expected 'Dungeon', got '{loaded.current_room}'"
assert loaded.health == 85, f'Expected health 85, got {loaded.health}'
assert loaded.gold == 120, f'Expected gold 120, got {loaded.gold}'
assert 'axe' in loaded.inventory, 'Inventory should contain axe'
assert 'shield' in loaded.inventory, 'Inventory should contain shield'
assert db.load_player('Nobody') is None, 'load_player should return None for missing player'
db.save_world_state('turn', '42')
assert db.load_world_state('turn') == '42', f"Expected '42', got '{db.load_world_state('turn')}'"
assert db.load_world_state('missing') == '', "load_world_state should return '' for missing key"
db.close()
acceptance_criteria: GameDatabase connects to SQLite and auto-creates tables on init
save_player() stores player name, health, room, gold, and inventory
load_player() returns a Player with all fields restored, or None
load_world_state() retrieves by key, returns empty string if missing
hints: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS for each table
save_player: INSERT OR REPLACE INTO players ..., then delete+reinsert inventory
load_player: SELECT from players, then SELECT item_id FROM inventory
save_world_state: INSERT OR REPLACE INTO world_state (key, value) VALUES (?, ?)
